description: PM-ratified exemptions for migrations whose down scripts are marked IRREVERSIBLE. Each entry proves the irreversibility determination was reviewed and approved. An IRREVERSIBLE down script without a corresponding entry here causes the migration-reversibility-gate to fail.

exemptions.reason: Data backfill from external_id resolution. NULL-to-value writes cannot be safely reversed without distinguishing backfill rows from application-written rows that postdate the migration. PITR is the only safe rollback path.

Three RPC functions replaced with fail-closed variants. The pre-920 function bodies re-expose fail-open behavior (dependent writes succeed even when owning pick transition affects zero rows). Restoration would re-introduce the known defect. PITR is the only safe rollback path.

Schema-only snapshot of the live database adopted as the single Live Schema Parity replay root (Option B baseline/squash). It is not a forward migration: its only 'down' would be dropping the entire public schema, and there is no prior state to restore to because every pre-baseline migration is archived as historical/non-replayable. Live recovery is PITR, never a down script.
